channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: A Physical Activity Clinical Practice Guideline for People With Moderate to Severe Traumatic Brain Injury
item.description: APTA has released a new Clinical Practice Guideline for Physical Activity in People with Moderate to Severe Traumatic Brain Injury.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Early Weight-Bearing Speeds Recovery After Unstable Ankle Fracture Surgery: study
item.description: A randomized controlled trial found that early weight-bearing and mobilization significantly speed recovery, improve functional outcomes, and progress rehabilitation after surgical fixation of unstable ankle fractures, without increasing complication rates.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials | BMC Urology
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials confirms the efficacy and safety of pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: APTA Report Finds Demand for Pelvic Health Physical Therapy Outpaces Access
item.description: A new APTA report, "APTA State of Pelvic Health Physical Therapy," reveals that demand for pelvic health physical therapy in the U.S. currently outpaces access, identifying key challenges in workforce, awareness, and timely care.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia: a systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis | Journal of NeuroEngineering and Rehabilitation
item.description: This systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis evaluated the effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Follow up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on paediatric ACL injuries: towards a broader vision of care
item.description: This editorial updates the 2018 IOC consensus on pediatric ACL injuries, incorporating new research on prevention, individualized management, evolving surgical strategies, and long-term holistic care, recognizing these injuries as a significant public health concern.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Multimodal Integration of Gait, Balance, and Infrared Thermography Enhances Machine-Learning Classification of Knee Osteoarthritis: A Cross-Sectional Study
item.description: A cross-sectional study investigated whether combining gait, balance, and infrared thermography data improves machine-learning discrimination between individuals with knee osteoarthritis and healthy controls compared to single-domain models.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) offers additional benefits over low-load exercise (LLE) and comparable benefits to high-load exercise (HLE) in muscle strength, disability, and pain for musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: This RCT evaluated the agreement in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions between physiotherapists and resident physicians compared with senior physicians as the reference standard in an emergency department for musculoskeletal complaints.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effects of physical exercise on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis: A systematic review.
item.description: This systematic review analyzed the effectiveness of different physical exercise interventions on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis, including randomized controlled trials.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Quantifying Physical Activity Through Step Count Estimation Across Multiple Wearable Sensor Configurations: A Validation Study in Post-Stroke and Healthy Adults.
item.description: This validation study identified the most accurate protocol for measuring stride-count in 18 post-stroke and 18 healthy individuals, comparing different wearable sensors, their placement, and data processing against a gold-standard instrumented treadmill.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: This study developed a core set of 9 relevant patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy, grouped into 3 domains: functioning, well-being, and participation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: This difference-in-differences analysis of 62,217 Medicare beneficiaries in the US determined the effect of bundled payment for inpatient lower extremity joint replacement (LEJR) on prehabilitation delivery.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial of 140 TIA/mild stroke patients found that a 6-week cardiovascular rehabilitation program significantly improved 6-minute walk test distance by 30 meters (p < 0.05) and moderate-to-vigorous physical activity over 6 months compared to usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating in athletes: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of studies from PubMed/MEDLINE, Scopus, Web of Science, and SportDiscus estimated the prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating among athletes across training levels and assessment methods, identifying associated moderators.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Prevention strategies and modifiable risk factors for upper extremity injury: a systematic review and meta-analysis for the female, woman and girl Athlete Injury pRevention (FAIR) consensus
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis identifies prevention strategies and modifiable risk factors for upper extremity injury in female, woman, and girl athletes as part of the FAIR consensus.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: The Impact of a Single Bout of Antagonist Stretching on Agonist Muscle Performance and Range of Motion: A Systematic review and Meta-analysis | Sports Medicine - Open
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis investigated the impact of a single bout of antagonist stretching on agonist muscle performance and range of motion, addressing conflicting findings in current literature.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Mental health in tennis: a cross-organisational hybrid summit-Delphi consensus process
item.description: A multi-method Delphi consensus process involving 83 participants from various tennis governing bodies generated recommendations for mental healthcare and wellness promotion among elite and junior tennis athletes.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Identifying Gaps in Rehabilitation Strategies for Chronic Stroke: A Systematic Review of Evidence in Patients With and Without Nerve Transfer.
item.description: This systematic review aimed to compare upper limb rehabilitation strategies and outcomes in chronic stroke patients with versus without contralateral C7 nerve transfer and identify key evidence gaps.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking in inpatient stroke rehabilitation: a multicentre cohort study.
item.description: A multicenter retrospective cohort study of 565 stroke patients determined subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking on discharge after inpatient rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed
